entry.title: How to Validate Business Partner’s CEP (Brazilian postal code) through Extensibility using ABAP
entry.content:
How to Validate Business Partner’s CEP (Brazilian postal code) through Extensibility using ABAP

SAP Solutions:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition (SAP Cloud ERP).
  • ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse.

Third party API:

              This blog utilizes the API https://viacep.com.br/. This is a free-to-use API provided by a third party, thus is not under any SAP responsibility. Keep in mind that its usage for CEP validation is at your own risk and that you can use any API of your choice.

Knowledge Required:

  • How to connect ADT for Eclipse to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
    • Connect to the ABAP System | SAP Help Portal
  • How to create an ABAP Class using ADT for Eclipse.
    • How to create a Class in ABAP 00 - 6 Steps to start | SAP Community
    • How to create a class in ABAP 00 - example code, with little typing. AdT is great! | SAP Community

Step I – Create an ABAP Class

              First of all, we need to create an ABAP class that makes the requests to the API. It should also handle the API response, which is in JSON format. Below, you will find a sample implementation of this class, but keep in mind that this is just an example and can be adjusted as needed.

CLASS zbl_cl_get_request DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_address_br,
             cep         TYPE c LENGTH 9,
             logradouro  TYPE c LENGTH 100,
             complemento TYPE c LENGTH 100,
             bairro      TYPE c LENGTH 60,
             localidade  TYPE c LENGTH 40,
             uf          TYPE c LENGTH 2,
             estado      TYPE c LENGTH 40,
             regiao      TYPE c LENGTH 20,
             ibge        TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             gia         TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             ddd         TYPE c LENGTH 3,
             siafi       TYPE c LENGTH 10,
             unidade     TYPE c LENGTH 50,
           END OF ty_address_br.

    TYPES: tt_address_br TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_address_br WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    CLASS-METHODS create
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_instance) TYPE REF TO zbl_cl_get_request.
    CLASS-METHODS get_data
      IMPORTING iv_postal_code TYPE string
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_data) TYPE ty_address_br .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ZBL_CL_GET_REQUEST IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_data.
    DATA ls_viacep TYPE ty_address_br.
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url(  'https://viacep.com.br'  ).
      CATCH cx_http_dest_provider_error INTO DATA(lo_http_dest_provider_error).
        "handle exception
        DATA(lv_http_dest_provider_error) = lo_http_dest_provider_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    TRY.
        DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( lo_destination ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO DATA(lo_web_http_client_error).
        "handle exception
        DATA(lv_web_http_client_error) = lo_web_http_client_error->get_text( ).
    ENDTRY.

    " Set HTTP Method (GET, POST, etc.)
    DATA(lo_request) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).
    DATA(lv_uri) = '/ws/' && iv_postal_code && '/json/'.
    lo_request->set_uri_path( lv_uri ).
    " Send Request
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_response) = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get ).
      CATCH cx_web_http_client_error INTO lo_web_http_client_error.
        "handle exception
        lv_web_http_client_error = lo_web_http_client_error->get_text( ).
        "handle exception
    ENDTRY.

    " Get Response
    DATA(lv_resp_satus) = lo_response->get_status( ).
    DATA(lv_resp_text) = lo_response->get_text( ).
    /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
      EXPORTING json = lv_resp_text
      CHANGING  data = ls_viacep
    ).
    rs_data = ls_viacep.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
    CREATE OBJECT ro_instance.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Step II – Enable this Class to be Used in Key-User Extensibility

              To use this class in the Custom Logic app inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the property “Use in Key User apps”, under “API State”, must be set as “Yes”. This must be done in ADT for Eclipse.

Step III – Implement the Custom Logic

  1. In the customizing tenant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, go to the Custom Logic app (F6957) and click on the “Create” button.
  2. Choose the BAdI “Validate BP BADI” (ID: “CMD_VALIDATE_BP”).
  3. Give it a name and description.
  4. Open the implementation and click on the button “Open Code Editor”.
  5. Add the sample code below.
  6. Publish the code, then start testing.

Again, this is just an example and can be adjusted as needed.

DATA: ls_bp_address LIKE LINE OF it_bp_address,
           ls_message    LIKE LINE OF ct_validationmessage,
           lv_message    TYPE string,
          lv_postalcode TYPE string.
*Clearing the variants, just in case
CLEAR: lv_message, lv_postalcode.
*Fiiling ls_bp_address with it_bp_address data
LOOP AT it_bp_address INTO ls_bp_address.
*Attributing the value for lv_postalcode
  lv_postalcode = ls_bp_address-postalcode.
*Removing the '-' from the CEP
  REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF SUBSTRING '-' IN lv_postalcode WITH ' '.
*Checking if the BP country is Brazil
  IF ( ls_bp_address-country = 'BR' OR ls_bp_address-country = 'Brazil' ).
    DATA(lo_srv) = zbl_cl_get_request=>create( ).
    DATA(ls_data) = lo_srv->get_data( iv_postal_code = lv_postalcode ).
    IF ls_data IS NOT INITIAL.
*Cheking if the region from the CEP's code list is differnent from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-uf = ls_bp_address-region.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |Region informed: { ls_bp_address-region }. Region CEP: { ls_data-uf }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
*Cheking if the city from the CEP's code list is differnt from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-localidade = ls_bp_address-cityname.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |City informed: { ls_bp_address-cityname }. City CEP: { ls_data-localidade }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
*Cheking if the street from the CEP's code list is differnt from the one informed in the BP Master Data
      IF NOT ls_data-logradouro = ls_bp_address-streetname.
        CLEAR ls_message.
        ls_message-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_message-msgv1 = |Street informed: { ls_bp_address-streetname }. Street CEP: { ls_data-logradouro }|.
        APPEND ls_message TO ct_validationmessage.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

Step IV – Transport

After testing, you can transport it.

  • Creating Extension Items and Transporting Them | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition – All Communication Scenarios in One Place (Bookmark)
entry.content:
Make sure to bookmark this blog so you can easily revisit all communication scenarios in one place. Share it with your friends and colleagues—it’s a handy resource when searching across different SAP portals.

What is a Communication Scenario ?

A Communication Scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is a predefined, packaged set of:

  • Inbound/Outbound APIs (OData, SOAP)
  • Business events (for event-based integration)
  • Message types
  • Authorizations

These scenarios act as the integration between SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and other SAP Products or external systems.

Think of a communication scenario as a “bundle” :

These are the APIs and permissions this external system is allowed to use, and here’s how the communication should happen.

Each Communication Scenario is delivered by SAP S/4HANA Public Product Release and cannot be modified—but you can activate and use them.

Key Components of a Communication Scenario

A typical scenario includes:

1. Communication User

A technical user used for authentication.
Supports:

  • Basic Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 (strongly recommended)

2. Communication System

Represents the external (non‑SAP) or SAP system you are integrating with.
Defines:

  • Hostname / Token URLs
  • Outbound target URLs
  • Authentication method

3. Communication Arrangement

This is where everything comes together.

You assign:

  • The Communication Scenario
  • The Communication System
  • The Communication User
  • Any specific parameters (e.g., service URLs, outbound destinations)

If you think of it simply:

Scenario = Template
Arrangement = Active Configuration

Why Are Communication Scenarios Important?

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, customers cannot create custom communication scenarios like in on‑premise or Private Cloud. This strict governance ensures:

  • Security and compliance
  • System stability
  • Standardized integration patterns
  • Ease of lifecycle management during upgrades

So instead of customizing scenarios, you configure and connect the right SAP‑delivered scenarios.

APIs usually come with a predefined communication scenario. To use the APIs, you first need to create a communication arrangement based on the communication scenario.

How to Configure a Communication Scenario: Step-by-Step

Step 1: Create a Communication User

SAP Fiori App: Maintain Communication Users

  • Create user → assign password → store securely
  • Or create an OAuth technical user (preferred)

Step 2: Create a Communication System

SAP Fiori App: Communication Systems

Here you:

  • Assign the communication user
  • Maintain authentication method
  • Provide target host details
  • Configure OAuth token endpoint (if applicable)

Step 3: Create Communication Arrangement

SAP Fiori App: Communication Arrangements

Steps:

  1. Select the communication scenario (e.g., SAP_COM_0008)
  2. Assign the communication system
  3. Review/activate inbound and outbound services
  4. Copy Service URLs for external system use

Once activated, the APIs are ready for use.

Testing Your Scenario

You can test APIs using tools like:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Hub)
  • SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
  • SAP API Management
  • SAP BTP  (Destinations, Side by Side Extensions, Extensibility Development)
  • Custom integrations

Check which communication scenario fits to your purposes best.

The following list gives you an overview of the most important communication scenarios available today

entry.title: Communication Arrangements in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Communication Arrangements

Communication Arrangements is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools.

With Communication Arrangements App,

  • Communication Arrangements details can be viewed.
  • Custom Communication Arrangements can be created with standard communication or custom communication scenarios using Communication Arrangements App.

Finding the Standard Communication Scenarios in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Choose the Product

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition

Navigate to APIs -> ODATA V2 / ODATA V4.

Search Required API

List of results for Purchase Order.

Purchase Order API Overview

API Resources

Navigate to API Resources -> Attributes -> Communication Scenario

API Specifications

Authentication Methods for API

Configuration Details for API

Extensibility

Custom Fields can be added to the API using below Business Contexts - Header and Items in Custom Fields App with Key User Extensibility.

API Reference

List of Entity Sets Available in the APIs.

Communication Scenarios

Navigate to Display Communication Scenarios,

All these are Standard Communication Scenarios.

Standard Communication Scenarios of Purchase Order - SAP_COM_0053

Steps to Create Communication Arrangements

Maintain Communication Users

Navigate to Maintain Communication Users App.

Maintain Communication Users is to create Communication Users for the API Access.

Choose New button to create Communication User

Add User Name and Password.

Propose Password can be chosen to generate password by system.

Save the Communication User.

If communication User is added to any Communication system or communication Arrangements, here it shows those details.

Communication System

Navigate to Communication System App.

Communication System is to maintain communication system with host name and communication User.

Provide Communication System ID and Name.

Add the Host Name

Add necessary details

Choose Add button to add Inbound Communication User.

Available Authentication Methods

Required details for SSL Client Certificate.

Required details for OAuth 2.0

Choose the Authentication Method, and choose User Name/Client ID.

Click on Ok after choosing Communication User.

Communication User is added.

 Communication System is saved.

Communication Arrangements

Navigate to Communication Arrangements app.

Communication Arrangements is to generate Inbound Service URLs by adding Communication System and Communication User.

Choose New Option to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Standard or Custom Communication Scenarios is required to create Custom Communication Arrangements.

Search for the Standard or Custom Communication Scenario

‘SAP_COM_0053’ is the Standard Communication Scenario for the Purchase Order API.

Arrangement appears automatically based on Communication Scenario and it can be changed.

Click on Create button to create Communication Arrangement.

List of Inbound Services available for that Communication Scenario.

WSDL/Service Metadata will be generated once Saved the Communication Arrangement.

Communication System and Communication User is required to create Communication Arrangements.

WSDL/Service Metadata is generated.

Service Metadata

Service URL is available in Communication Arrangement.

Access the Service URLs through the Communication User details.

Conclusion

standard communication or custom communication scenarios are used to create Communication Arrangements.

In Communication Arrangements, OData URLs are generated based on inbound Service.

References

  • Communication Arrangements | SAP Help Portal
  • Maintain Communication Users | SAP Help Portal
  • Communication Systems | SAP Help Portal
  • Integration | SAP Help Portal
  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: On-Stack Partner Reference App: A Guide to Clean-Core Extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
As SAP partners and customers continue to innovate in the SAP cloud ERP space, one principle remains non-negotiable: keep the core clean. While SAP’s extensibility guidelines often favor side-by-side builds on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), some scenarios require tighter, real-time integration with core processes. For these tightly coupled cases, on-stack extensibility within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides the essential solution. It offers a powerful way to enhance standard business processes—without modifying SAP-delivered code—enabling end users to innovate securely and at scale.

To bridge theory and practice, the On-Stack Partner Reference Application provides a practical blueprint for building these clean-core extensions. Using modern ABAP and RAP principles, it demonstrates how to extend standard SAP applications in a secure, upgrade-stable, and scalable way, fully aligned with SAP guidelines.

 When to Choose On-Stack Extensibility

While a side-by-side architecture on SAP Business Technology Platform is a powerful approach, it isn’t the ideal fit for every business need. Sometimes, an extension must be tightly woven into the main system to deliver the best results. On-stack extensibility is the recommended path for these tightly coupled scenarios, especially when your solution requires:

  • Deep integration with standard business processes
  • Seamless navigation across SAP Fiori applications
  • A harmonized user experience
  • Enhancements that are upgrade-safe within the same system

On-stack extensibility allows developers and organizations to achieve these goals by building directly within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud stack. This approach preserves the clean-core architecture, ensuring the system remains stable and maintainable. The Partner Reference Application demonstrates the recommended “golden path” for achieving this perfect balance.

The Reference Scenario: Loyalty Hub

When on-stack extensibility is the right approach, the On-Stack Partner Reference Application provides a hands-on example that demonstrates how to extend standard SAP processes securely and efficiently. It goes beyond theory to present a full end-to-end scenario that partners can explore, adapt, and use as a practical blueprint.

The scenario uses a Loyalty Hub application integrated with the standard Manage Sales Orders – Version 2 SAP Fiori application. Its goal is to help organizations manage customer loyalty programs directly within sales operations, while preserving seamless navigation between standard SAP functionality and the custom extension.

To make the scenario concrete, we demonstrate the application using two personas:

  • Aarti, the loyalty administrator, manages memberships, awards loyalty points, and issues gift cards
  • Julia, the sold-to party or customer, earns points, redeems rewards, and applies gift cards during sales transactions

This approach highlights both administrative and customer perspectives, showing how Loyalty Hub supports real-world workflows and interactions. Here’s how the Loyalty Hub works from both the administrative and customer perspectives:

Key Capabilities

The application delivers comprehensive loyalty management:

  • Handles memberships, tiers, and related transactions
  • Tracks and awards loyalty points automatically in response to sales order events
  • Issues and redeems gift cards tied to customer orders
  • Sends automated notifications for points updates and tier changes
  • Supports both administrator workflows and end-user interactions

Technical and Operational Design

  • Built with ABAP RAP and custom business objects in the Z namespace
  • Data modeled using CDS, ensuring separation from standard SAP objects and upgrade stability
  • User interfaces designed with SAP Fiori elements, supporting flexible extensions and semantic navigation
  • SAP-standard authentication and role-based authorization for secure access
  • Operational readiness includes scheduled jobs for membership evaluation, number range handling for controlled object creation, and application logging/messaging for monitoring and troubleshooting

This scenario illustrates how partners can innovate within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud stack, extending functionality while preserving clean-core principles, upgrade safety, and a consistent SAP user experience — all while reflecting both administrative and customer perspectives.

Key Business Value

The reference application helps your organization accelerate development while staying fully compliant with clean-core principles. By using it as a guide, your teams can:

  • Gain a reference architecture aligned with SAP standards.
  • Use proven patterns to integrate with standard applications.
  • Learn to build reusable and deployable solutions.
  • Accelerate the delivery of robust and upgrade-safe extensions.

The Loyalty Hub demonstrates that innovation and compliance can coexist—adding real business value without compromising the SAP clean core. The best way to understand the power of this approach is to see it in action.

You can get started and try the Loyalty Hub example by accessing the complete reference application on GitHub. By following this framework, you can confidently deliver the innovative, compliant, and upgrade-safe solutions your business requires.

On Stack Partner Reference Extension for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Explore Other Reference Applications

Depending on your extensibility strategy, you can also explore:

  • Partner Reference Application for SAP BTP ABAP , Blog
  • Cross-Stack Partner Reference Extension
  • Partner Reference Application for SAP BTP and CAP

Together, these resources guide developers and architects across on-stack, side-by-side, and cross-stack extension patterns—helping them choose the right architecture while staying aligned with SAP’s clean-core and cloud-first principles.

entry.title: Manage the pending quantities to confirm in the Sales Order - RAP BO Events I_SALESORDERTP
entry.content:
This requirement is to be implemented in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

The requirement is to manage the pending quantities to be confirmed in the sales order. New items should be added to the sales order for unconfirmed quantities, and the existing item quantity should be updated according to the confirmed quantity.

The standard functionality in SAP Sales LoB allows users to use a specific item category to create a purchase requisition for a specific material. Therefore, after a new line item is added to the sales order, a purchase requisition can be created for that item category within the sales order.

Pre-requisites : 

  • Required Business Roles must be assigned to the user. 
  • Configuration for creation of purchase requisition for specific item category in Sales Order.

Solution: 

When a sales order is created in the S/4 system, the standard event “Sales Order Created” is raised with the following fields: EventRaisedDateTime, SalesOrder, SalesOrderType, SalesOrganization, DistributionChannel, OrganizationDivision, and SoldToParty.

The Sales Order Created event can be locally consumed within the same system in which it is raised. This local consumption is implemented with the help of an event handler class. The event handler class is set up as a class pool, similar to a behavior pool.

Refer SAP help documents used in this solution:

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/business-event-consumption

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_SALESORDERTP

Local Consumption ( DEV system Client 080 - ABAP Development Toolkit):

  1. To register a local consumption, custom ABAP Class is created and RAP BO i_salesordertp (name of behaviour definition of RAP BO) is specified.

    CLASS zcl_salesord_local_event_handl DEFINITION
    PUBLIC
    FINAL
    CREATE PUBLIC

    FOR EVENTS OF i_salesordertp.

    PUBLIC SECTION.
    PROTECTED SECTION.
    PRIVATE

  2.  Next, need to create a local handler class under Local Types of Global custom class which inherits from standard class CL_ABAP_BEHAVIOR_EVENT_HANDLER.

    CLASS lcl_so_event_cons DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
    PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
    consume_create FOR ENTITY EVENT created_instances FOR salesorder~created.

    ENDCLASS.
    Note: SALESORDER~CREATED, which is stated after the FOR word.

    CREATED_INSTANCES is the name of importing payload which would have event data.
  3.  Released CDS view i_salesorderitem can be used to get additional data  - confddelivqtyinorderqtyunit, requestedquantity and other required fields. 
  4. For each instance o sales order items,  compare the REQUESTEDQUANTITY with CONFDDELIVQTYINORDERQTYUNIT and update Sales order as follows:
    • If both quantities are same – no update required.
    • If CONFDDELIVQTYINORDERQTYUNIT quantity is less then REQUESTEDQUANTITY then modify Entities I_SALESORDERTP and create new item by passing, quantity, product and category to CBAB.                                                                                                                                        MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp
      ENTITY salesorder
      CREATE BY \_item
      * FROM lt_create "lt_sales_order_items
      FIELDS ( product
      requestedquantity salesorderitemcategory )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_so_item1 IN lt_so_items
      (
      salesorder = ls_so_item1-salesorder
      %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = ls_so_item1-soitem
      product = ls_so_item1-product
      requestedquantity = ls_so_item1-so_quan
      salesorderitemcategory = ls_so_item1-item_cat ) ) ) )
      MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped)
      FAILED DATA(ls_failed)
      REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).
    • If there is no CONFDDELIVQTYINORDERQTYUNIT then update existing sales order item for category CBAB.
      MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp ENTITY salesorderitem
      UPDATE
      FIELDS ( salesorderitemcategory requestedquantity )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_so_upd1 IN lt_so_items_upd
      ( requestedquantity = ls_so_upd1-so_quan
      salesorderitemcategory = ls_so_upd1-item_cat
      %key-salesorder = ls_so_upd1-salesorder
      %key-salesorderitem = ls_so_upd1-soitem ) )
      FAILED DATA(ls_failed_upd)
      REPORTED DATA(ls_reported_upd).
  5. The above custom class will be executed after the sales order is committed in the S/4 system and will update the sales order as required. A locking issue may occur if the sales order is opened again in change mode before the consumption class execution is completed.                        

Troubleshooting:  Local Custom class created in ADT for event consumption can be debugged easily, and any issues encountered during support can be resolved more efficiently.

Conclusion:  In this use case, if we need to update application data within the system and no data needs to be retrieved from external sources, then this can be handled via local consumption of events and solution can be implemented using a RAP-based BO approach. There is no need to consume the event outside the system and then trigger the custom integration flow in the Integration Suite in BTP.

entry.title: Accessing Your Financial Data: G/L Account Line Items API
entry.content:
If you're working with SAP's Finance module, the G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X) API offers a straightforward way to retrieve journal entry information. This service pulls data from the universal journal, giving you access to key accounting fields alongside cost accounting objects.

What You Can Retrieve

The API allows you to access balances across multiple areas including General Ledger Accounting, Asset Accounting, Management Accounting, Margin Analysis, and Material Ledger. You can pull three types of balances:

  • Opening balances show totals from the fiscal year start date up to your selected date, including balance carryforward.
  • Moving balances capture all line items posted within a specific time interval, excluding carried-forward balances—useful for analyzing quarterly movements or custom time periods.
  • Ending balances combine opening balances with changes (credits and debits) within your selected timeframe.

Important Limitations

This API isn't designed for full data extraction. The universal journal contains enormous data volumes, so the service exposes only a subset of fields. We recommend using aggregation and applying strong filters to your requests.

If you need line-item level detail, include the following key fields, for example,

  • AccountingDocument,

  • CompanyCode,

  • FiscalYear,

  • Ledger,

  • LedgerGLLineItem, (ACDOCA line item number)

  • SourceLedger,

Note that AccountingDocumentItem (ACDOCA-BUZEI) is only used for associations to BSEG views.

The API also cannot retrieve cumulative ending balances.

You'll find this service on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, where you can explore example requests and additional documentation to help you get started with your financial data queries. The image shows the API on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub:

More information

Here's where you find additional useful information:

G/L Account Line Items - Read (A2X)

Communication Management

 APIs in Finance

APIs for General Ledger Accounting

entry.title: Dos and Don’ts: Key User Custom Fields in Add-Ons Based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Have you ever been in a situation where you developed an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Scalable Delivery add-on and you wanted to add extension fields extending SAP standard objects to your add-on? Are you wondering how to do that and what to consider?
If your answer is yes, then this blog post is just for you. In this blog, we explain which options you have and what to consider in this situation.

Key User Custom Fields vs Developer Extensibility Custom Fields

When adding extension fields to an SAP standard object in your scalable delivery add-on (from now on, we’ll use add-on as abbreviation for scalable delivery add-on) you can choose to use:

  1. key user custom fields or
  2. developer extensibility custom fields.

The pros and cons for and against the two options are discussed in this blog:
Custom Fields in S/4HANA: Key User versus Developer Extensibility
Please note that beyond the pros and cons listed in this blog, you may choose “developer extensibility only”, for example, to keep the software logistics of your add-on as simple as possible.

In addition, consider that the scope of the SAP standard applications enabled for key user custom fields is much broader than the scope of those enabled for developer extensibility custom fields. If you choose “developer extensibility only”, make sure that you really only need to extend SAP standard applications which are enabled for developer extensibility custom fields.

The scope limitations of developer extensibility custom field-enabling are:

  • developer extensibility custom fields are only supported for RAP-based applications
  • developer extensibility custom fields do not support SAP GUI UIs
  • developer extensibility custom fields do not support SOAP APIs

Please note that when you create key user custom fields for an add-on, one more software component is needed that’s exclusively used for key user extensions. It’s created automatically. This happens when you create a namespace and activate key user extensibility for this namespace using the Maintain Namespaces app (details are explained below in the “preparation steps” section).  Remember to maintain key user custom fields in the Custom Fields app and in the customizing tenant, and not via ABAP Development Tools (hereafter called “ADT”) in the development tenant.

Before you can start creating key user custom fields for your add-on, there are some preparation steps needed. Let’s have a look at these preparation steps in more detail.

Preparation steps
1. First, you need a namespace. In the Landscape Portal, go to the Maintain Namespaces app. Here, you’ll see a list of namespaces you already have, or you can request a new namespace. In the latter case, you need an S-user for your company which has authorization to create namespaces.


Request namespace

In the same app, install the namespace in your development system. This is needed to make sure that all the key user custom              fields you want to include in your add-on utilize this namespace. You need to install the namespace in your test system, too, to              be able to transport your development into your test environment to validate it.

2. Next, log on to your development tenant and go to the Manage Software Components app. Within the app, you can create a new software component for your namespace which contains your developer extensibility objects. We’ll call this software component “developer extensibility software component”.


Create the developer extensibility software component

Afterwards, you need to clone this new software component within the same app. This automatically creates the following objects based on the entered name, which can be used in ADT for your Cloud development:

  1. developer extensibility software component (e.g. /S4PET/CLOUDDEV)
  2. structure package (e.g. /S4PET/CLOUDDEV)

Now, log on to your customizing tenant and go to the  Extensibility Settings app where you can see all available namespaces for key user extensibility. To create custom fields in your namespace, you must activate key user extensibility for the given namespace by selecting the “Activate Key User Extensibility” button. Enter a name for the key user software component that will be created within the namespace.

Activate key user extensibility and assign a user to this namespace

Click “Confirm”. Based on the name you’ve entered, the following objects are created for your key user development and automatically assigned to a software collection:

  1. key user extensibility software component (e.g. /S4PET/KEYUSER)
  2. key user extensibility development package (e.g. /S4PET/KEYUSER_EXT)
  3. key user extensibility software component relation (e.g. /S4PET/KEYUSER)
  4. software collection (e.g. /S4PET/KEYUSER)

In addition, you must assign your user to a namespace. If you don’t do this, your key user custom fields will still have “YY1_” as a namespace and won’t be included in the add-on.

 

3. To be able to create your own developer extensibility objects (e.g. an ABAP class) for your add-on, you first need to create a new development package in ADT in the development tenant underneath your structure package (this was automatically created during preparation step 2).  

Create developer extensibility development package

To be able to program against your key user custom fields from your developer extensibility objects, you must declare a software component relation from your developer extensibility software component to your key user extensibility software component. Just create a "Software Component Relation" object within the structure package. The object must have the same name as your developer extensibility software component where your objects will be located, and from which you want to access your key user custom fields.


Declare software component relation in your developer extensibility software component

Within this software component relation object, you maintain a dependency on your objects in the key user extensibility software component (see also Using Software Components). If you don’t do this, you will get ATC errors in all your developer extensibility objects which reference key user custom fields.

4. If you plan to use your key user custom field in a developer extensibility behavior definition (BDEF) object to specify field control for this key user custom field, you should additionally also maintain the software component relation for the key user extensibility software component (automatically created during preparation step 2). There, you must maintain access permissions for the developer extensibility software component in ADT in the development tenant.

Walking through an example step by step

Now that you’re done with the preparation work, learn how you can use key user custom fields in your add-on following a simple example:
We want to create an ABAP class which selects all Sales Documents from the SAP-delivered Sales Document CDS view. After that, we want to extend this CDS view with a new custom field and select this field.

1. In ADT (development tenant), a new ABAP class is created within the development package (see preparation step 4). This ABAP class contains a simple select on CDS view I_SalesDocument and prints out the sales document ID in the console.

Example developer extensibility class

2. Log on to your customizing tenant and open the Custom Fields app. In our example, we create a new custom field of type Checkbox for the Sales Document business context in the namespace (see preparation step 3).

Create key user custom field

As soon as the business context Sales Document gets extended by a new custom field, the CDS view I_SalesDocument gets extended by this field, too (this has been configured by SAP in that way).



Enable the custom field for CDS view I_SalesDocument 

3. In ADT (development tenant), you can now consume the key user custom field that you’ve created within the ABAP class you created earlier.


Consume the key user custom field in your developer extensibility class

4. Without a dependency declaration in the developer extensibility software relation component (as described in preparation step 4) you will either receive errors by the syntax check on your ABAP class, or by running the ATC check directly. The error message could then look like this: “CDS Entity <name of CDS extend> of software component <name of key user software component> is used without declared dependency.”


Error messages when dependency declaration in the developer extensibility software relation component is missing

To fix the errors, you need to go to the developer extensibility software component relation that you created earlier (see preparation step 4) and add a dependency to the key user software component where the custom field resides.


Dependency declaration in the developer extensibility software relation component

5. Now that you’re done with development, the next step is to release everything and test it in the test system afterwards. First, transport the custom field. To do that, log on to the customizing tenant and open the Export Software Collection app. There, search for the key user software collection created in preparation step 3 (e.g. /S4PET/KEYUSER). The key user software component relation that was created automatically and the development package are already part of the software collection. Add the key user custom field via the “Add Items” button. After that, export it into the test system. When the export is finished, go back to ADT (development tenant) and release the transport request with the developer extensibility objects (containing e.g. the developer extensibility software component relation, ABAP class).

6. The next step is to import into the test system. First, log on to the test tenant and navigate to the Import Collection app. In the list of software collections, find your key user software collection (Type: “Key User Extensibility”) and the developer software collection (Type: “Developer Extensibility”).
First, you need to import version 0 of the key user software collection (containing only the software component) by clicking “Check and Import”. Next, version 0 of the developer software collection needs to be imported. When this is done, you can continue to import the other versions (containing the objects you’ve created) of the key user software collection and the developer software collection. When the imports are finished, test your development in the test system.

7. When everything is tested successfully, the next step is to register and build (Deliver a Product) the add-on product in the landscape portal. The commit IDs that you need can be found in the “Import Collection” app in the details for the imported software collection under “Execution Messages”. For the developer extensibility software component, the commit IDs can also be found in the “Manage Software Components” app.

Now that you know how to use key user custom fields in an add-on, let’s go ahead and have a look at the things you should avoid doing.

Don’ts

Within the key user extensibility scope, only key user custom fields can be delivered as part of an add-on. Other key user extension types are not supported. As a consequence, you can't maintain features in the “Custom Fields” app that depend on other key user extension types such as custom business objects or custom business logic.

One of the issues that may occur is that you may accidentally define the software component relation incorrect or not at all. If this is the case, you’ll get ATC errors when you build your add-on product. This needs to be fixed as described above.

Another prominent issue is that the respective key user custom fields may not have been assigned to the key user software collection before exporting, or that the transport request with the developer extensibility objects that consume the custom fields has been released and imported first. This results in syntax errors in the target system. In this case, export and import the key user software collection with the respective custom fields. Then, the syntax errors will disappear.

Please note that all objects need to be active. It’s not possible to export a software collection which contains an unpublished key user custom field. Additionally, it’s not possible to release a transport request in ADT which contains inactive objects.

Do’s

Another problem that can occur is that SAP may have adjusted the content enabled for key user custom fields, and because of that, you need to republish your affected key user custom fields. Scalable delivery add-ons can be used within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only, and all involved (partner and customer) systems will be upgraded with each SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release.

In general, you need to build a new version of your add-on product once for every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release so that your add-on is always installable for customers.

Technical changes by SAP on extended SAP objects (e.g. a migration from V1 views to V2 views) may require that custom fields in the add-on product need to be adapted. In this case, you will automatically get a so-called rework task for the impacted fields within the Custom Fields app to republish the fields. After republishing, you have to export the software collection and build a new add-on version containing the respective changes.

Rework task for the impacted fields

That’s it! Congratulations!
You successfully made your way through the Do’s and Don’ts of Key User Custom Fields @ SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition scalable add-ons.

Please note that this blog has been elaborated by a team of experts. I want to thank Yasmina van Rooij, Christian Fuhlbruegge, René Dehn, Kai Dehmann, and Mario Dietrich Collada for their extremely helpful contributions.

Feedback, Comments, or Questions:

You’re cordially invited to provide feedback, comments, or questions in the comments section below. Please follow our profiles to get updates when we publish more posts on these topics.
Feel invited to post and answer questions here (https://answers.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26) and read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/270c4f37-c335-46e1-bfad-a256637d5e26/).

Other related blog posts and SAP help documents:

  1. Developing an Extension Using Key User Extensibility
  2. Developing ABAP Extensions Using Scalable Delivery
  3. Software Component Relations
  4. Extensibility - General Availability of Scalable Solution for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  5. Developing an Extension Using Key User Extensibility

entry.title: Disable Credit Check in Custom Sales Process
entry.content:
Background

As shared in a previous blog, it is possible to create custom sales order types in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This new innovation provides greater flexibility to fulfill customer business requirements in the Order‑to‑Cash process.
In this blog, I will share a real business use case and explain how to utilize custom sales order types to address customer requirements.

Customer Requirement

Supply company XYZ sells various products to customers. For certain customers, the credit check is required when selling high‑value products to ensure that sufficient credit limits are available before processing the sales order. However, for low‑value products, a credit check is not required in order to reduce the additional operational effort involved in order processing. The company XYZ also wants to have reporting to analyze sales data of these sales processes. 

Since only the standard sales order type “OR” is available to support the sell‑from‑stock process, a single sales order type is not sufficient to differentiate between sales processes for high‑value products and low‑value products. In addition, the credit check configuration is maintained at the sales document type level. Therefore, having different sales order types is a logical approach to support these distinct business use cases.

Solution 

Let's see how custom sales order type fulfills this requirement. 

Assume you are on SAP CBC and already finished the activity "Define Scope" and "Set Up Organization Structure". Now you can open the activity "Configure Standard Order Type" to define a new custom sales order type. 

Create a custom sales order type by copying standard type template XTA. 

Save and confirm the custom sales order type. Then deploy new custom sales order types to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Wait until the deployment is complete. 

Once custom sales order types are deployed to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can open the activity "Configure Your Business Processes", and search for SSCUI 102137 "Set Credit Limit Checks for Sales Document Types".

System will navigate to the SSCUI in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to configure the credit limit checks for sales document type. Find the entry of custom sales order type ZCRD and set the setting "Check Credit" to empty, which means no credit check. 

Save the configuration in SSCUI, and now the credit check is disabled for custom sales order type ZCRD. 

Log on with user "Internal Sales Representative" and open the app "Create Sales Order" to create a sales order with custom sales order type ZCRD. 

The sold-to party 10100005 is the customer who has credit limit maintained in the master data. 

Add the sales order item and save the sales order. 

Once sales order is saved, go to tab "Status and Blocks" and you will see this sales order is not blocked by credit check.

You can continue creating outbound delivery from the sales orders with custom sales order type ZCRD because no credit check is performed for such sales orders. 

Let's compare with the standard sales order type "OR". Create a sales order with sales order type "OR".

Use same sold-to party "10100005".

Add the item and save the sales order. A popup message is shown that credit limit is exceeded.

Go to tab "Status and Blocks", you will see the sales order with standard order type OR is blocked by credit check.

You need to work with Credit Controller to release the DCD (Documented Credit Decision) to get rid of the credit block on sales order to proceed the subsequent logistics processes. 

Conclusion

The introduction of custom sales order types is a significant innovation that greatly enhances flexibility in the Order‑to‑Cash process. It allows customers to define their own sales processes with minimal configuration effort to support different sales scenarios and process patterns.

With a single click, all relevant configurations from the standard sales order type “OR” are copied to the new custom sales order type, enabling customers to immediately execute the sell‑from‑stock process without any additional fine‑tuning. This capability empowers customers to adapt their sales processes more efficiently while reducing implementation complexity.

Please stay tuned for more template copy use cases, and feel free to submit your requirements through the Customer Influence Portal to help us prioritize our product roadmap.

entry.title: Dynamic Watermark in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Output Management: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Recently, I got one of those requirements that sounds incredibly simple on paper but turns into an interesting technical puzzle once you work on S4HPC.

The business wanted a conditional watermark on their Purchase Order Output Form. If the PO is still navigating the approval workflow, it needs to display a massive "DRAFT" watermark across all pages. Once fully approved and released, that watermark needs to disappear completely.

The idea is straightforward: add custom logic to make the watermark conditional at both, form template level and the data source level. But as anyone who works with SAP Output Management knows, getting the Master Form and Content Form to talk to each other isn't always plug-and-play.

Here is how I did it.

Step 1: The Data Source Dilemma

Since the watermark needs to appear on all pages, the logical place to put it is the master form template.

First, I checked the standard PO form data source FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV to see if I could simply grab the PurchasingProcessingStatus and ReleaseIsNotCompleted fields of I_PurchaseOrderAPI01 view to build my condition. Unsurprisingly, they weren't there.

My next thought was to extend the standard data source via the Custom Fields app. However, FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV cannot be extended.

After some digging, I came across SAP Note 3525721, which states that custom fields can be used for the PO output data source with the use BAdI MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT.

 

Step 2: Adding the Custom Field via BAdI 

First, I went to the Custom Fields app (Business Context: Procurement: Purchasing Document) and created a new Text(1) or Indicator field called YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH. I made sure to enable its usage for the respective Form Templates.

Next, I implemented the BAdI MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT to determine the workflow status. Here is the BAdI logic:

SELECT SINGLE FROM i_purchaseorderapi01 WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS
  FIELDS 
  WHERE purchaseorder = @purchaseorder
    AND releaseisnotcompleted = @ABAP_false
    AND purchasingprocessingstatus = '05'
  INTO (po_approved).
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  purdoc_extension-yy1_po_wf_approved_pdh = 'X'.
ENDIF.

 After downloading the Content Form XML/XDP again, the newly added custom field was sitting right there in the Data View palette.

Step 3: Form Layout Setup

Now, let’s jump into Adobe LiveCycle Designer to actually build the layout.

I added the DRAFT image inside a subform (named formWatermark) on both of the Master Pages: mst1 (the first page) and mst2 (subsequent pages).

Note: Make sure your formWatermark subform is set to Positioned (not Flowed) in the Object palette, and right-click to select Send to Back. This ensures it acts as a true background watermark and doesn't push your main content areas down.

Here is where we hit the major roadblock: How do we access the master page watermark subform from the content form? Or conversely, how do we access the content form data from the master page?

This can be achieved from either side using a simple xfa.resolveNode trick. You only need to use one of the following methods/approaches.

Note: For both approaches, ensure your Script Editor dropdowns are set to Language: JavaScript and Run At: Server. In S/4HANA Public Cloud, form rendering happens on the Server. 

Method #1: Controlling it from the Master Template Form

To access our custom field value from the Content template form dataset within the Master template form, we need the absolute path of the field from the Content template's data view (e.g., PurchaseOrderNode.YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH).

We concatenate xfa.datasets.data.Form. with our custom field path. Place this JavaScript logic on the formWatermark subform's initialize event:

var WFStatusNode = xfa.resolveNode("xfa.datasets.data.Form.PurchaseOrderNode.YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH");

if (WFStatusNode.value === "X") {
    this.presence = "hidden";
} else {
    this.presence = "visible";
}

Method #2: Controlling it from the Content Template Form

Alternatively, we can access the master page watermark subforms directly from within the content template form by prefixing the path with #pageSet[0]..

Place this JavaScript code on the YY1_PO_WF_APPROVED_PDH field's initialize event:

var firstPageWatermarkNode = xfa.resolveNode("#pageSet[0].mst1.formWatermark");
var subsequentPageWatermarkNode = xfa.resolveNode("#pageSet[0].mst2.formWatermark");

if (this.rawValue === "X") {
    firstPageWatermarkNode.presence = "hidden";
    subsequentPageWatermarkNode.presence = "hidden";
} else {
    firstPageWatermarkNode.presence = "visible";
    subsequentPageWatermarkNode.presence = "visible";
}

The Final Result

After choosing your preferred approach, upload the forms (Master and Content) back into the system and test your output.

When the PO is still in approval:

And once the approval is fully completed:

The draft watermark completely vanishes!

It’s a relatively simple requirement that forces you to touch a few different areas of S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility, combining the MMPUR_PO_OUTPUT_HEADER_EXT BAdI with a little Adobe Forms JavaScript gets the job done perfectly.

Have you tackled similar form requirements in Public Cloud recently? Let me know your approach in the comments.

entry.title: Custom Logic using Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

In On Stack extensibility, Enhancements can be created using Custom Logic App in Key User Extensibility.

Key user extensibility also know as In App extensibility, extensions can be added with key user tools.

 

Key User Extensibility / In App Extensibility- Custom Logic

Custom Logic is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools                        

In the Custom logic App, 

  • Custom logic can be added
  • Easy exploration of extension points, key user documentation
  • Syntax check, syntax highlighting, code completion
  • Draft, published, testing included
  • ABAP Language Version for Key Users
  • Enhancements can be created using Custom Logi App

Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Business Accelerator Hub provides a section for On Stack extensibility (key user extensibility and developer extensibility), to know list of SAP CDS views, APIs, Business Add-ins etc. are available with the release status.

Navigate to Business Accelerator Hub to find BAdis

On Stack Extensibility -> Business Add-ins

Search for required BAdi.

This BAdi is related to check purchase order before saving to create it.

Here it shows BAdi Definition and Enhancement spot.

BAdi can be used in both Key User and Developer Extensibility as both states of BAdi is in released state.

Methods and its Parameters

 

Steps to Create Custom Logic

Navigate to Custom logic App

Custom Logic App

Choose Create button to create Custom Logic.

Choose Required Business Content

 

List of available BAdi Definitions for the business context.

Choose required BAdi Definition.

Its Documentation.

Choose Step 2 to add Implementation Description and ID.

Implementation ID automatically appears based on Description and it can also be changed.

Choose Review

Choose Create

Click on Publish button.

Its published.

Click on Open Code Editor to write the logic.

Importing Parameters.

Changing Parameters.

Sampe Code -> Show Sample Code

Keyboard Shortcuts -> three dot menu -> Show Keyboard Shortcuts

Write the required logic.

IF purchaseorder-supplier EQ '0000100005'.
APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E' messagevariable1 = |Choose another Supplier| ) TO messages.
ENDIF.

 Navigate to Create Purchase order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check.

Error is appeared as per logic written.

This implementation class can be opened in the eclipse, and break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

Key-user objects can be displayed and debugged but cannot be edited with ABAP development tools.

 

Conclusion                     

In Key User Extensibility,

Custom logic can be added and Enhancements can be created using Custom Logic App.

Own implementation can be created and those can be deleted.

Implementation can be published to test, production systems

Code can be compared with older code versions.

 

References

  • Custom Logic | SAP Help Portal
  • Custom Logic Tracing | SAP Help Portal
  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Key User Extensibility | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: BAdi Enhancement Implementation using Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In On Stack extensibility, Enhancements can be created using BAdi Enhancement Implementation in Developer Extensibility.

Developer extensibility using SAP Cloud ABAP Environment for developer extensions.

 

Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Business Accelerator Hub provides a section for On Stack extensibility (key user extensibility and developer extensibility), to know list of SAP CDS views, APIs, Business Add-ins etc. are available with the release status.

On Stack Extensibility -> Business Add-ins

Search for required BAdi.

This BAdi is related to check purchase order before saving to create it.

Here it shows BAdi Definition and Enhancement spot.

BAdi can be used in both Key User and Developer Extensibility as both states of BAdi is in released state.

Methods and its Parameters

Developer Extensibility – BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Steps to Create Impelementation

Create New BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Chose BAdi Enhancement Implementation.

Add Name and description.

Add Enhancement Spot

BAdi Enhancement Implementation is created.

Choose BAdi Implementation to add Implementations.

Add BAdi Definition and Custom Class name

Add Implementation Class

Choose Implementation Class, add Custom Class name and its description

Implementation Class is created.

 

ZCL_CHECK_PO▸
CLASS zcl_check_po DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
   
   INTERFACES if_badi_interface
   INTERFACES if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po .

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_check_po IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po~check. 
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Implementation Class is added to Badi Implementation details

Logic can be added to implementation class

 

ZCL_CHECK_PO▸
CLASS zcl_check_po DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
   
   INTERFACES if_badi_interface
   INTERFACES if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po .

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_check_po IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_ex_mmpur_final_check_po~check. 
    IF purchaseorder-supplier EQ '0000100005'. 
      APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E' messagevariable1= |Choose another Supplier] ) TO messages.
    ENDIF.  
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

Navigate to Create Purchase Order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check

Error appeared as per logic written.

Add breakpoint in the implementation class to debug.

Place cursor on the logic, choose right click.

Break point is added

Break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

Conclusion

In Developer Extensibility, BAdi Enhancement Implementation can be created and deleted.

Custom Implementation can be captured in TR's and those can be moved to test, production systems.

This custom implementation can also be debugged.

References

  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Developer Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Implementing BAdI Enhancement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Setting Mandatory Fields in Purchase Requisitions via Custom Logic in Public Cloud
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Purchase Requisitions (PRs) often require additional field validations to ensure data completeness and process compliance. While some fields can be defined as mandatory through standard customizing, this is limited to predefined fields and specific scenarios.

This blog post explains how to set additional standard fields as mandatory in Purchase Requisitions by using Custom Logic with Key User Extensibility.

The solution applies to the following apps:

  • Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional
  • Create Purchase Requisition – Advanced

Business Requirement

The business requirement was to enforce mandatory entry of the following fields at purchase requisition item level:

  • Purchasing Organization (Field EKORG)
  • Requisitioner (Field AFNAM)

Why standard customizing is not sufficient

Using the customizing activity Maintain Account Assignment Categories (ID 101815), some fields (for example Cost Center) can be set as mandatory. However:

  • This applies only during the creation of an account assignment category
  • Only predefined fields are available
  • Additional standard fields cannot be enforced

To cover these gaps, Custom Logic (BAdIs) must be used.

Solution Overview

To fulfill the requirements, two different Custom Logic extension points are used:

Both extension points are available via the Custom Logic app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Custom Logic 1: Change of Field Control (Purchasing Organization)

Extension Point: Change of Field Control for Purchase Requisitions

Extension Point ID: MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE

Description

This BAdI allows you to:

  • Hide fields
  • Set fields as read-only
  • Set fields as mandatory
  • Set fields as optional

It works directly at UI field control level.

⚠️Limitation
This extension point supports the field PURREQNSSPREQUESTOR, which is relevant only for Self‑Service Procurement.
It does not enforce the Requisitioner field in apps Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional or Create Purchase Requisition - Advanced. Therefore, a second custom logic is needed for the field Requisitioner.

Sample Code –  Change of Field Control (Purchasing Organization) 

* Sample Code

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs> TYPE mmpur_s_pr_fldsel.


   READ TABLE fieldselection_table ASSIGNING <fs> WITH KEY field = 'PURCHASINGORGANIZATION'.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
     <fs>-fieldstatus = '+'.
    ENDIF.

* END OF Sample Code
 
✔️  Result:
  • The Purchasing Organization field becomes mandatory on PR item level.

Custom Logic 2: Check of Purchase Requisition Item (Requisitioner)

Since the Requisitioner (AFNAM) cannot be enforced via field control, a check-based validation is required.

Extension Point: Check of Purchase Requisition Item

Extension Point ID: MM_PUR_S4_PR_CHECK

Description

This BAdI allows you to:

  • Perform custom validations
  • Trigger error messages
  • Prevent saving the Purchase Requisition

Sample Code – Check of Purchase Requisition Item (Requisitioner)

* Sample Code

DATA: ls_message LIKE LINE OF messages.

IF PURCHASEREQUISITION-PURCHASINGDOCUMENTTYPE = 'NB'.
LOOP AT purchaserequisitionitem_table INTO DATA(ls_item).
      IF ls_item-PURREQNREQUESTOR = ''.

         APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = 'E'
         messageid = 'ME'
         messagenumber = '999'
         messagevariable1 = 'Enter Requisitioner / Anforderer fehlt' ) TO messages.
         purchaserequisitionhaserror = abap_true.
         RETURN.
      ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

* END OF Sample Code

 

✔️  Result:
  • The PR cannot be saved if the Requisitioner is missing
  • A clear error message is displayed to the user

Transport

Custom Logic implementations are not transported automatically and must be added to a Software Collection.

Steps

  1. Open Export Software Collection
  2. Select an existing collection or create a new one
  3. Choose Add Items
  4. Filter by:
    • Last Changed On
    • Last Changed By
  5. Add the Custom Logic entries
  6. Transport the collection as usual

Summary

By combining two Custom Logic extension points, it is possible to:

  • Enforce mandatory fields beyond standard customizing limits
  • Apply validations consistently across professional and advanced PR apps
  • Stay fully within SAP-recommended extensibility

This approach provides a flexible and upgrade-safe way to enhance Purchase Requisition quality.

References

  • 3345421 - BAdI: Field Control for Purchase Requisitions - SAP for Me
  • How to Manage and Export Your Software Collection | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: How to Use Custom Fields in Event-Based Settlement for Assets Under Construction
entry.content:
Co-author: H. Trefftz

Learn how custom fields can be used in the event-based settlement process from cost posting through to final asset reporting.

Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508, you can use event-based settlement to settle investment projects to assets under construction.

Release 2602 takes this further by introducing custom field support throughout the entire settlement process. You can use app extensibility and define custom fields that you can then use throughout the whole event-based settlement process: You can post to the custom fields in the cost posting, for example, when posting an invoice. The custom fields are also posted to in the settlement postings: The custom field values are inherited when the WBS element is settled to the asset under construction (AuC) and also when the AuC is settled to the completed asset. Finally, the customer fields are also available in reporting. This means better tracking and more meaningful reporting in both General Ledger Accounting and Asset Accounting.

Using an example, you are guided through the entire process - from the definition of custom fields to reporting in Asset Accounting. This example additionally contains the substitution of a custom field with another field.

Step 1: Preparation - Define and Enable Custom Fields

In our example, we want to create two custom fields for the following purposes:

Custom Field,
Name

Custom Field,
Technical Name

Purpose

AUC Assignment

YY1_AUC_COBL

Have an input field for user input in the following apps:

  • Post General Journal Entries (F0718) and
  • Manage Supplier Invoice (F0859)

AUC User Plant

YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM

During the posting to the WBS element of an investment project, we want to transfer the content of the Plant field to this custom field. As a result, we have the information about the original plant available throughout the whole process and see its value in the settlement journal entry.

We define our custom fields in the Custom Fields app.

When defining the custom fields, we use the following business contexts:

  • Accounting: Coding Block (FINS_CODING_BLOCK) and
  • Accounting: Journal Entry Item (FINS_JOURNAL_ENTRY_ITEM)

After we have published the custom fields, the list of custom fields looks as shown in Figure 1.

Figure 1: List of custom fields with their business contexts (Custom Fields app)

We then need to enable the field for usage in our apps. We do this on the User Interfaces tab of the Custom Fields app.

  • To have the custom fields available in the posting apps and in the substitution app, the corresponding OData services need to be enabled.
  • To make the custom fields available in the reports of the Asset Transactions app, we at least need to enable the fields for the OData Service FAA_ASSET_TRANSACTIONS_SRV.

In our example, we enable our first custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the input in both posting apps and the asset transactional reporting. This is shown in Figures 2a-d.

Figure 2a: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the Manage Journal Entries app

Figure 2b: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the apps Post General Journal Entries, Display Line Items in General Ledger, and the Journal CDS view

Figure 2c: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the app Manage Supplier Invoice

Figure 2d: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the Asset Transactions app

Furthermore, we enable our second custom field (YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM) for substitution and for reporting in the Asset Transactions app:

Figure 3a: Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM for the app Manage Substitution and Validation Rules (F4406), Variant Journal Entries

Figure 3b: Same as figure 2d. - Enabling custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM for the Asset Transactions app (F1614)

If we wanted to use the custom fields in further apps, we would have to enable the corresponding services, too.

Step 2: Preparation - Define Substitution Rule for Custom Field

We use the app Manage Substitution and Validation Rules (F4406), variant Journal Entries, to define the substitution for our second custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM. During a cost posting to the WBS element, the system shall replace the custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM with the Plant entry.

Figure 4a: Creating the substitution rule, entry screen

 

Figure 4b: Creating the substitution rule, detail screen

After we had created the substitution rule, we have activated it.

Step 3: Preparation - Create Investment Project and AUC for Event-Based Settlement

A final preparation step is still missing before we can use the custom fields in the settlement process: We create an investment project with a WBS element, assigning an event-based settlement profile.

We create the investment project IM_EB1 in the app Project Control - Enterprise Projects (F3215), assign the settlement profile EB0001, and then release the project.

Figure 5: Creating the investment project (in app Project Control - Enterprise Projects (F3215))

The system automatically creates a corresponding asset under construction (AuC).

We then create a settlement rule for final settlement from the AuC to the completed asset (1010-20000000-0).

Step 4: Posting - Make Postings with Custom Field Input

Substep 4-1: Post General Journal Entry

Since we have enabled the custom field YY1_AUC_COBL for the required OData services, we can enter values for this custom field in the posting apps when posting costs to the WBS element.

In our example, we make a posting in the app Post General Journal Entries (F0718) where we enter the following account assignments:

  • WBS element: IM_EB1
  • Custom field AUC Assignment (YY1_AUC_COBL): Input 1

Figure 6: Posting in app Post General Journal Entries (F0718)

Substep 4-2: Post Supplier Invoice

We make a second posting in the Manage Supplier Invoice app (F0859) where we enter the following account assignments:

  • WBS element: IM_EB1
  • Custom field AUC Assignment (YY1_AUC_COBL): Input 2

Figure 7: Posting in app Manage Supplier Invoice (F0859)

Substep 4-3: Post Purchase Order with 'Plant' Substitution and Subsequent Goods Receipt

We create a purchase order for our investment project (WBS element).

We do this in the app Manage Purchase Orders (F0842A), where we specify the following:

  • Account Assignment Category: Project (P)
  • Plant: New Plant FIO1 (FIO1)
  • WBS Element: IM_EB1

Figure 8: Creating a purchase order (in app Manage Purchase Orders (F0842A))

Later, we post the goods receipt that belongs to the purchase order. We do this in the app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843); we specify the purchase order.

Figure 9: Posting the goods receipt (in app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843))

The cost posting is then made to the WBS element IM_EB1. Since we have created the investment project (with WBS element IM_EB1) with an event-based settlement profile, the costs are settled directly to the AuC. The process flow is shown in figure 10.

Figure 10: Process flow from purchase order to AuC

As defined in the substitution rule, our second custom field YY1_AUC_JE_ITEM is replaced with the plant entry of the goods receipt journal entry (5000000001) and the custom field is also copied to the settlement journal entry (100000003).

Figure 11: Line items for the goods receipt journal entry (in app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843))

Figure 12: Line items for the settlement journal entry (in app Manage Journal Entries (F0717A)

Step 5: Posting - Post Final Settlement to Completed Asset

During the settlement of the AuC to the final receiver asset (completed asset) 20000000-0, all custom field information is taken over to the line items for the AuC retirement posting on the one hand and to the line items for the asset acquisition (for the completed asset) on the other.

Figure 13: Posting of final settlement from AuC to completed asset

Step 6: Reporting

For reporting purposes, you can use the Asset Transaction app (F1614). In its view Journal Entry Tracing for Settlement, you can display the settlement values for both, for the AuC and for the completed asset including the custom field account assignments.

Figure 14: Reporting the settlement postings including the custom field account assignments (app Asset Transaction (F1614), view Journal Entry Tracing for Settlement)

Conclusion and More Information

Custom fields flow seamlessly through the entire settlement process, while data consistency is ensured. Whether you're tracking specific cost categories, plant assignments, or other custom-specific data, custom fields make it possible.

The setup of custom fields requires enabling OData services and may involve creating substitution rules, but once configured, the process runs smoothly and automatically.

Want to learn more about event-based settlement? - For more information, see the help portal documentation under Assets Under Construction: Management and Settlement, Event-Based Settlement | SAP Help Portal and Extensibility: Using Custom Fields with Event-Based Settlement

For general information about the enablement of custom fields in the accounting business contexts as well as for UI adaptions, see SAP Note 3448139.

entry.title: How to Add Custom Field in Purchase Requisition Item in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Step-by-Step)
entry.content:
Business Requirement

In one of my clients, there is a requirement where we need to capture Department information at the Purchase Requisition (PR) Item level for:

  • Internal tracking
  • Reporting & analytics

However, this field is not available as standard in SAP Public Cloud.

Solution

There is a tile called Custom Fields, where we can create a custom field, and then we can assign it to PR. The detailed steps are below:

Step 1: Create Custom Field

> Navigate to the Custom Fields App as shown below:

Create a new field as shown below :

  • Label: DEPARTMENT

  • Identifier: YY1_DEPARTMENT

  • Business Context:
    Procurement: Purchase Requisition Item (MM_PURREQN_ITEM)

  • Field Type: Code List

Here, we are choosing codel because it fulfils my requirement where we can assigned muliplte pre defined value, like how we did in the ECC & S/4 Hana on-prem when creating a domain:

Step 2: Maintain Code List Values

Define department values:

Code Description

ACCAccounts
AUDAudit
ENGEngineering
ITIT
MKT1Marketing-I
MKT2Marketing-II
SSStock & Store

This allows dropdown/value help in UI

Step 3: Maintain UI Texts

Configure labels properly:

  • Label: DEPARTMENT (Desc.)

  • Short Label: Dept.

  • Medium Label: Dept.D

This ensures proper display across UI screens.

Step 4: Enable Field Usage

Go to the User Interfaces tab and enable usage in:

CDS Views

  • C_EXTPURCHASEREQUISITIONITEM

  • I_PURCHASEREQUISITION_API01

  • I_PURCHASEREQUISITIONITEMAPI01

SAP GUI

  • PR_ITEM_GUI (Purchase Requisition Item GUI)

This is a very important step (most people miss this)

Step 5: Publish the Field

Click Publish

Once published:

  • Field is added to the database structure

  • Available for UI & API consumption

Step 6: Test in Purchase Requisition

Open Purchase Requisition screen.

New Tab Appears:

Department

Inside tab:

  • Field: Department

  • Value Help enabled

Behaviour in System

  • User selects department from the dropdown

  • Values are controlled via Code List

  • Field is stored at the PR Item level

Key Technical Points

  • Field is automatically extended in:

    • CDS Views

    • OData Services

    • UI annotations

  • No need for manual ABAP coding

  • Fully compliant with the SAP Public Cloud extensibility model

Advantages

✔️ No modification required
✔️ Works in both SAP GUI & Fiori
✔️ Reusable across APIs & reports
✔️ Easy maintenance via Code List

Common Mistakes to Avoid

❌ Not enabling CDS views
❌ Forgetting SAP GUI enablement
❌ Not publishing after changes
❌ Incorrect business context selection

Conclusion

Custom Fields in SAP Public Cloud provide a powerful, upgrade-safe way to enhance standard applications.

With minimal effort, we successfully:
✔️Added Department field
✔️Enabled dropdown values
✔️Integrated with UI & backend

This approach can be reused for multiple business requirements across procurement and finance.

Personal Experience:

If this requirement for ECC or S/4 Hana on-prem, we could have done it in the old traditional way. But this public cloud experience is on another level, the satisfaction I got while doing the research about it can be compared to how we used to search user exits and customer exits at first time when entering into SAP. Even tho SAP has restricted most things for public cloud, it also provides us an easy solution, as we can see it here. There is one more app called Custom Logic, where we can write code for the validation, which we will discuss later.

Please feel free to connect if you have any queries.

Happy Learning 😀

entry.title: How to Buffer Master Data in Sales Documents to Improve Performance
entry.content:
Overview

In this blog post, you'll learn how to buffer master data during sales document processing to avoid unnecessary CDS view or database calls. These calls can have a negative impact on API calls or apps. 

Buffering can be implemented either using pure key user tools or through developer extensibility. In this post, we’ll walk you through both options.

 

Example

Consider a typical scenario: You want to read the Customer ABC classification from I_CustomerSalesArea for the ship-to party of each item in a sales order.

When you create a sales order, the Modification of Item Custom Fields in Sales Documents Business Add-In (BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM) is usually called three times per item. For a sales order with 1,000 items, that means 3,000 BAdI executions.

If your BAdI implementation reads master data from a CDS view in every single call, you hit the database thousands of times, even though the data you read is identical across many items.

In a sales order with 1,000 items and 5 different ship-to parties, the implementation reads the database up to 3,000 times, when 5 times would be enough.

 

Implementation Concept

The solution is buffering: Store the result of each unique lookup in the memory and reuse it on subsequent calls. This can be achieved using static buffering in a custom class. When you use a static attribute for the class, the buffer is kept for the entire duration of the Logical Unit of Work (LUW) and is shared across all BAdI calls within the same request.

The pattern is simple:

1. A static internal table holds results that were already read, keyed by the lookup key.
2. A static method checks the buffer first. If the entry exists, it returns it immediately. If not, it reads from the CDS view, stores the result in the buffer, and then returns it.

 

Implementation Steps

1. Create Buffer Class

a. Key User Tools

Custom libraries are reusable ABAP classes created in the Custom Reusable Elements app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  1. In the Custom Reusable Elements app, create a new custom library:

    This generates a global ABAP class (in our example, YY1_BUFFER) with language version 2.

  2. Define a structure type for the customer sales area buffer as follows:

    Component IDTypeLength
    CUSTOMERText10
    SALESORGANIZATIONText4
    DISTRIBUTIONCHANNELText2
    DIVISIONText2
    ABCCLASSIFICATIONText2
     
  3. Define structures, tables, and data as follows:

     

  4. Define a new method GetABCClassification() with the following parameter:


  5. Implement the method as follows:
*Check internal buffer
Read table yy1_buffer=>abc_classifications with key
    customer = customer
    distributionchannel = distributionchannel
    division = division
    salesorganization = salesorganization
        assigning field-symbol(<abc_classification>).

IF sy-subrc = 0.
    ABCclassification = <abc_classification>-abcclassification.
    RETURN.
ENDIF.

" Cache miss — read from CDS view
SELECT SINGLE
CustomerABCClassification
FROM I_CustomerSalesArea
WHERE Customer = 
AND SalesOrganization = @salesorganization
AND DistributionChannel = @distributionchannel
AND Division = @division
INTO @abcclassification.

" Store in buffer — also stores empty result to avoid repeated failed lookups
INSERT value #(
customer = customer
salesorganization = salesorganization
distributionchannel = distributionchannel
division = division
abcclassification = abcclassification
) INTO TABLE yy1_buffer=>abc_classifications.
​
